channel.title: Hanta Hub — Live MV Hondius Hantavirus Outbreak
channel.description: Live updates on the 2026 MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak — case counts, regional risk, prevention guides, and news source aggregation.

item.title: Day 13 of Monitoring: No New PCR-Positives Across Receiving Countries in Five Days, UNMC Cohort Asymptomatic Through May 31, French ECMO Patient Status Unchanged, Cluster Holds at 12
item.description: Monday May 25, 2026 is Day 13 of the WHO 42-day active monitoring window for the MV Hondius cohort. The cluster total holds at 12 cases (10 PCR-confirmed Andes virus, 2 probable) and 3 deaths — 23 days since the last fatality. No new PCR-positive results in any receiving country for five days; WHO characterises the cluster as stabilising.

item.title: Hantavirus in Dogs and Pets: What the Published Evidence Actually Shows About Animal Susceptibility, Household Risk and Practical Steps for Pet Owners
item.description: Can dogs and cats catch hantavirus, carry it asymptomatically, or transmit it back to humans? The published evidence is consistent: dogs do not become clinically ill from hantavirus, and there is no documented pet-to-human transmission. Practical risk-reduction steps for households in endemic regions.

item.title: What Happens After Day 42: The Operational Step-Down From WHO Active Monitoring to Post-Window Surveillance for the MV Hondius Cohort
item.description: The WHO 42-day active monitoring window closes on June 21. What actually happens on Day 43, why the receiving-country institutions step down rather than stop, and the 28-day passive-surveillance tail that follows.

item.title: Hantavirus and Property Values: What Buyers, Sellers and Vacation-Rental Owners Should Actually Know About Andes Virus and Sin Nombre Virus Disclosure in Endemic Regions
item.description: Does hantavirus depress property values? What should a buyer ask during inspection? The honest, evidence-based answers for households and vacation-rental operators in Andes virus and Sin Nombre virus endemic regions.

item.title: Hantavirus Mortality Rate by Country — Andes, Sin Nombre, Hantaan, Puumala and Dobrava CFR Compared
item.description: Hantavirus case-fatality rates vary enormously by strain, country, and care-system maturity. A country-by-country and strain-by-strain comparison of Andes, Sin Nombre, Hantaan, Seoul, Puumala and Dobrava CFRs.

item.title: Cluster Rises to 10: French Passenger Worsens in Paris, US Cohort Lands in Nebraska
item.description: Two more passengers from the MV Hondius — a French woman repatriated to Paris and an American man flown to Nebraska — tested positive for hantavirus Monday, lifting the cluster to 10 cases. The WHO has now formally recommended a 42-day active monitoring window for everyone who disembarked.

item.title: Why 42 Days? The Andes Virus Incubation Window Explained
item.description: The WHO's newly formalised 42-day quarantine recommendation for MV Hondius passengers is a direct reflection of how Andes virus actually behaves inside the human body. Here is what virologists and public-health officials know about the upper bound of that incubation window — and why it isn't arbitrary.

item.title: MV Hondius Docks at Tenerife · Disembarkation Underway · First Repatriation Flight to Madrid
item.description: The Dutch-flagged expedition ship arrived at Granadilla port at 5:30 a.m. local time on Sunday, beginning a 24-hour disembarkation operation. The first repatriation jet, carrying 13 Spanish passengers and one Spanish crew member, has departed for Madrid where passengers will be quarantined at the Gómez Ulla military hospital.

item.title: Inside Madrid Quarantine: What the Day-7 PCR Means For Returning MV Hondius Passengers
item.description: Why the Spanish health ministry is using a two-PCR protocol — one on arrival and a second one a week later — for returning MV Hondius passengers, what an asymptomatic positive would actually mean, and what the rest of us can take from it.

item.title: MV Hondius Approaches Tenerife as Suspected Ninth Hantavirus Case Tests Negative
item.description: A suspected ninth case has been ruled out, leaving the MV Hondius cluster at eight cases and three deaths. The ship is expected to dock at Granadilla port in the early hours of Sunday as the WHO Director-General arrives in Tenerife to oversee disembarkation.

item.title: For Tenerife Residents and Travellers: What the MV Hondius Arrival Actually Means for You
item.description: The MV Hondius is approaching the Canary Islands, with passengers due to disembark at Granadilla port on Sunday. Here is a calm, practical look at what the arrival does — and does not — mean for residents, port workers, and visitors.

item.title: CDC Activates Level 3 Emergency Response as Hantavirus Cluster Reaches Nine Cases
item.description: A suspected ninth case has surfaced on Tristan da Cunha, two laboratories on two continents have independently confirmed Andes virus, and the US Centers for Disease Control has activated its Emergency Operations Center as the MV Hondius sets course for Tenerife.

item.title: Two Labs, One Verdict: Why Independent Andes Virus Confirmation Matters
item.description: South Africa's NICD and Switzerland's Geneva University Hospitals have independently confirmed Andes hantavirus in MV Hondius samples. Here is why two-laboratory confirmation is not just bureaucratic — it is scientifically meaningful.

item.title: Eight Cases, Eight Countries: WHO Expands Hantavirus Contact Tracing Across Three Continents
item.description: A newly confirmed eighth case has pushed the MV Hondius cluster into one of the most internationally tracked hantavirus events on record. Eight nations are now coordinating with WHO, and US states have begun monitoring returned passengers.

item.title: Returned from a South Atlantic Cruise? A Practical Self-Monitoring Guide for the Next Eight Weeks
item.description: You probably weren’t on the MV Hondius. But if you were on any expedition cruise to South Georgia, Tristan da Cunha, or Patagonia in March or April, here is exactly how to monitor yourself — and when to act.
